instructions.instruction: Use file= instead of path= when targeting a note by name; file= resolves like a wikilink (name only, no path or extension needed), while path= requires the exact vault-root path including extension
instructions.original_snippets: file=<name> — resolves like a wikilink (name only, no path or extension needed); path=<path> — exact path from vault root, e.g. folder/note.md
instructions.relevant_when: Reading, updating, or appending to an Obsidian note by its name

instructions.instruction: Always quote parameter values that contain spaces; unquoted values with spaces are parsed as separate tokens and the command will fail or target the wrong note
instructions.original_snippets: Quote values with spaces: obsidian create name="My Note" content="Hello world"
instructions.relevant_when: Passing any note name, content, or path parameter that may contain spaces

instructions.instruction: Use the silent flag when creating or updating files from an agent workflow to prevent Obsidian from opening the file in the editor and disrupting the user's current view
instructions.original_snippets: Use silent to prevent files from opening
instructions.relevant_when: Creating, updating, or appending to a note when the user has not explicitly asked to view the result

instructions.instruction: Specify vault=<name> as the first parameter when the user has multiple vaults open and vault identity matters; the default target is the most recently focused vault which can be unpredictable
instructions.original_snippets: Commands target the most recently focused vault by default. Use vault=<name> as the first parameter to target a specific vault
instructions.relevant_when: The user has multiple Obsidian vaults open or mentions a specific vault by name

instructions.instruction: Run obsidian help to discover all currently available commands; the output is always up to date and more reliable than guessing command names from memory
instructions.original_snippets: Run obsidian help to see all available commands. This is always up to date.
instructions.relevant_when: Unsure whether a command exists or what its exact syntax is

instructions.instruction: Use obsidian eval only during plugin development and debugging, never in production plugin code; eval runs arbitrary JavaScript in the app context and is a development tool only
instructions.original_snippets: Run JavaScript in the app context: obsidian eval code="app.vault.getFiles().length"
instructions.relevant_when: Developing or debugging an Obsidian plugin and needing to inspect app state

instructions.instruction: Follow the develop/test cycle when iterating on plugin code: reload the plugin with plugin:reload, check for errors with dev:errors, then verify visually with dev:screenshot or dev:dom
instructions.original_snippets: Reload the plugin to pick up changes: obsidian plugin:reload id=my-plugin; Check for errors: obsidian dev:errors; Verify visually with a screenshot or DOM inspection
instructions.relevant_when: Making changes to an Obsidian plugin and needing to verify them

instructions.instruction: Use \n for newline and \t for tab when providing multiline content in a single CLI parameter value
instructions.original_snippets: For multiline content use \n for newline and \t for tab
instructions.relevant_when: Creating or appending notes with structured content such as headings, lists, or tables
